customer_anxiety_profile: Scam-aware, cost-focused first-timer. Casey named scam anxiety explicitly and framed it as a generational shared experience. The concern was parked by Holland's personal empathy but not structurally resolved — the closer should expect it to resurface and should anchor early on the customer-initiated nature of the inquiry.
unresolved_concerns: Scam anxiety: partially addressed by Holland's personal empathy, not resolved by structural legitimacy anchors. Closer should reframe the appointment as customer-initiated.

Net metering preference: Casey explicitly wants to stay connected to Southside Electric for net metering and grid backup. Closer must confirm this is the design intent before presenting any off-grid framing.
Backup power concern: Casey raised generator dependency as a reason to stay grid-connected. This is a Powerwall cross-sell signal the closer can pick up — Holland did not surface it.

Cost transparency: Casey asked twice about upfront cost and monthly payment. Holland gave program-level framing without numbers. Closer should expect Casey to re-ask and should have the design numbers ready to anchor the conversation.
Bill not yet received: The appointment cannot be scheduled until Casey sends the bill screenshot. The closer should confirm bill receipt before the sit is locked.
sensitive_topics_raised: Scam anxiety — Casey named it directly and connected it to Holland's apparent age ('you feel like a young person too'). Tone matters: peer-level, not corporate.

Parents' solar farm contract — Casey's parents have a commercial solar farm contract on the same property. Closer should clarify whether this affects Casey's home system eligibility or net metering setup.
Barn/converted-barn property type — atypical structure on a shared parcel with sibling's home. Roof type and age were never captured. Closer should flag for the design team before the appointment.
Fixed pole charge ($100/month) — Casey pays a fixed infrastructure charge to Southside Electric regardless of consumption. This is the primary bill-pain anchor; closer should lead with it.
exact_promises_rep_made: Holland will send a text with instructions to screenshot the Southside Electric bill showing 12 months of kWh usage.
An appointment will be scheduled after the bill screenshot is received.
Casey can stay connected to Southside Electric for net metering — Holland confirmed this is possible.
closer_should_know: This appointment is not yet booked — it is conditional on Casey sending a bill screenshot. The call ended cooperatively but with no time anchor and no button-up. Casey is scam-aware, cost-focused, and has a specific preference to stay grid-connected for net metering and backup power. The $100/month fixed pole charge is the sharpest bill-pain anchor on the call and was never emotionally activated by Holland — the closer should lead with it. Roof type and age were never captured on what Casey described as a barn; flag for the design team before the sit.
